es.example: Con todo respeto, no estoy de acuerdo.
es.exampleTranslation: With all due respect, I disagree.
es.regionNote: Peninsular usage often keeps the article (con todo el respeto); LatAm default is bare. Reviewer to confirm.

it.exampleTranslation: With all due respect, I disagree.

bridge.note: The article war again, in the politest possible sentence: Italian respects THE respect, Spanish respects it bare. The disagreement that follows is identical.

interference.es_to_it: Spanish drops the article; Italian insists: con tutto IL rispetto.
interference.it_to_es: Italians add it: 'con todo el respeto' — heard in Spain, but the pan-Hispanic fixed form is bare.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm. Cue drafted by Claude 2026-07-20 from the entry's meaning and bridge — English situational framing, not a language claim, so it needs editorial taste rather than native review. Override freely.
